item.title: Nobody needs over $100,000 per year in Social Security benefits
item.description: Capping benefits for the wealthiest seniors is a good starting point for reforming the program.

item.title: Useful idiots visit Cuba, right on time
item.description: Communism has impoverished the island. American leftists only care now because they can blame Trump.

item.title: A streetcar no one desired
item.description: The white elephant project will run for the final time on March 31.

item.title: Outnumbered
item.description: Michael Ramirez cartoon on the U.S. economy, Iran war and gas prices.

item.title: Fallout from ‘The Population Bomb’
item.description: Paul Ehrlich and the Suez Crisis, in readers’ eyes.

item.title: In their game of chicken, Trump and Iran tap the brakes at last
item.description: But a pause is not a peace, and the real victims are still waiting.

item.title: Iran’s cheap, deadly drones have done the U.S. a favor
item.description: The Pentagon surely knows that the era of relying solely on exquisite, expensive weapons is over.

item.title: Is Netanyahu actually injured? I answered your questions.
item.description: David’s live chat with readers started at 12 p.m. ET on Monday. Read the transcript.

item.title: So long, farewell, Kennedy Center. Let’s put on a happy face.
item.description: With the upcoming Kennedy Center closure, D.C. nightlife needs a cultural infusion.

item.title: Why the Afroman ‘pound cake’ police saga is so delectable
item.description: Thin-skinned police, rap videos and pound cake aside, this ridiculous saga is also seriously inspiring.

item.title: Only a certain type of leader wants his face everywhere
item.description: Dictators always want to cement their legacy. But nothing lasts forever.

item.title: Don’t blame Trump’s stupid war on Israel
item.description: The Jewish state, as usual, makes a handy fall guy. The president might look in the mirror instead.

item.title: Gretchen Whitmer’s failed literacy test
item.description: The Michigan governor wants to fix literacy rates, but won’t let schools hold back students who need it.

item.title: Americans shouldn’t have to see data centers on their electricity bill
item.description: Public utilities aren’t equipped to deal with the massive increase in energy demands from AI.

item.title: The ugly truth about ‘merit’ promotion in the federal government
item.description: GS rankings, wastewater treatment and Congress, in readers’ eyes.

item.title: Why Poland really became a top 20 economy
item.description: It’s a free-market success story.

item.title: Behind the scenes, a compromise on TSA takes shape
item.description: Sending ICE agents to airports won’t help as much as ending the shutdown.

item.title: D.C.’s war on renters
item.description: Tenant “protections” that invite rampant abuse aren’t good for anyone except squatters.

item.title: You don’t want to miss the tall ships of the 250th. These photos show why.
item.description: These sailing vessels have drawn huge crowds since the Bicentennial.

item.title: The case against the Senate filibuster no Republican can make
item.description: How to put Congress, not the president, in the U.S. politics driver’s seat: End the 60-vote tradition.

item.title: Cancel culture is bad for business. Bluesky is learning why.
item.description: The social media platform just raised $100M but faces a major barrier to growth.
